content.text:
Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.",
  "totalSteps": 13,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
    "2. Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java",
    "3. Create PineconeClient.java + PineconeClientTest.java",
    "4. Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",
    "5. Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks",
    "6. Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java",
    "7. Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java",
    "8. Add RAG DTOs and new AiController endpoints",
    "9. Create frontend API client and hooks for RAG endpoints",
    "10. Create QueryAnswerCard.tsx + QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx",
    "11. Create SemanticSearchInput.tsx + SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx",
    "12. Create InsightPanel.tsx + InsightPanel.test.tsx",
    "13. Wire InsightPanel and SemanticSearchInput into MyWeek.tsx and TeamWeek.tsx"
  ]
}

Step 1: Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml
{
  "id": "step-1",
  "title": "Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
  "description": "1. Add `@EnableAsync` to `Application.java` (alongside existing `@EnableScheduling`).\n2. Add `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`, `ai.pinecone.environment`, and `ai.embedding.model`/`ai.embedding.dimensions` entries to `backend/src/test/resources/application.yml` (with blank api-key so tests never hit real APIs).\n3. Verify that the main `application.yml` already has these entries (it does — just confirm they're complete).\n4. Create Flyway migration `V10__add_ai_suggestion_team_week.sql` to add `team_id UUID REFERENCES team(id)` and `week_start_date DATE` columns to the `ai_suggestion` table. Add index: `CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_team_week ON ai_suggestion (team_id, week_start_date, suggestion_type)`. This is needed because TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows must be queryable by team+week.\n5. Update `AiSuggestion.java` entity to add `teamId` (UUID, column `team_id`) and `weekStartDate` (LocalDate, column `week_start_date`) fields with getters/setters.\n6. Update `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` to accept two new optional parameters (`UUID teamId`, `LocalDate weekStartDate`) via an overloaded method. The new overload calls through to the existing one then sets the two new fields before save. This avoids breaking existing callers.\n7. Add new query methods to `AiSuggestionRepository`: `List<AiSuggestion> findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(UUID teamId, LocalDate weekStartDate, String suggestionType)` and `List<AiSuggestion> findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(UUID planId, String suggestionType)` (already exists).",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

This is relay round 2. Another agent has already worked on this step.

You are in VERIFICATION MODE. Your default action is to APPROVE.
Focus your review on the DIFF — the changes made in the previous round.
Only make changes if there is a MATERIAL defect: failing check, missing step requirement, logic bug, or security issue.
Style preferences, alternative approaches, comment wording, and optional polish are NOT grounds for changes.

If all checks pass and the step requirements are met, approve immediately.
Log anything else you notice as observations — do not act on them.

Previous agent's notes:
Build and all tests pass. Here's a summary of everything reviewed and changed:

## Summary

### What I reviewed
- `Application.java` — had `@EnableScheduling` but was missing `@EnableAsync`
- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` — already had all `ai.pinecone.*` and `ai.embedding.*` entries (confirmed complete)
- `backend/src/test/resources/application.yml` — was missing `ai.pinecone.*` and `ai.embedding.*` entries
- `AiSuggestion.java` — was missing `teamId`/`weekStartDate` fields
- `AiSuggestionService.java` — only had the 6-arg `storeSuggestion()` method
- `AiSuggestionRepository.java` — was missing the `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType` method
- Flyway migrations — highest existing was V9, V10 needed to be created

### Changes made

1. **`Application.java`** — Added `@EnableAsync` annotation alongside existing `@EnableScheduling`
2. **`src/test/resources/application.yml`** — Added `ai.pinecone` (api-key: "", index-name, environment) and `ai.embedding` (model, dimensions) entries
3. **`V10__add_ai_suggestion_team_week.sql`** — New Flyway migration adding `team_id UUID REFERENCES team(id)` and `week_start_date DATE` columns plus composite index `idx_ai_suggestion_team_week`
4. **`AiSuggestion.java`** — Added `teamId` (UUID, `team_id`) and `weekStartDate` (LocalDate, `week_start_date`) fields with getters/setters; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
5. **`AiSuggestionService.java`** — Added 8-arg overload of `storeSuggestion()` that delegates to the existing 6-arg method then sets `teamId`/`weekStartDate`; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
6. **`AiSuggestionRepository.java`** — Added `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType` derived query method; added `java.time.LocalDate` import
7. **Ran `./gradlew spotlessApply`** to fix formatting violations in `AiSuggestionService.java`

All checks pass: `build` ✅ and `unit` ✅.

Verdict: changes_made

.dockerignore

spring:
  application:
    name: weekly-commit
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/weekly_commit
    username: ${DB_USER:postgres}
    password: ${DB_PASSWORD:postgres}
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: none   # Flyway owns the schema; Hibernate should not re-validate it
    open-in-view: false
  flyway:
    enabled: true

server:
  port: 8080

# ── OpenAPI / Swagger UI ───────────────────────────────────────
springdoc:
  api-docs:
    path: /api-docs               # JSON spec at /api-docs
  swagger-ui:
    path: /swagger-ui.html        # UI at /swagger-ui.html
    tags-sorter: alpha
    operations-sorter: alpha

# ── Actuator & Observability ───────────────────────────────────
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: when-authorized
  metrics:
    tags:
      application: ${spring.application.name}

# ── Feature Flags ──────────────────────────────────────────────
app:
  scheduling:
    enabled: ${APP_SCHEDULING_ENABLED:true}
  features:
    ai-assistance: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
    notifications: ${FEATURE_NOTIFICATIONS:true}

# ── AI Provider ────────────────────────────────────────────────
ai:
  enabled: ${FEATURE_AI_ASSISTANCE:true}
  provider: ${AI_PROVIDER:openrouter}   # "openrouter" or "stub"
  openrouter:
    api-key: ${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:}
    model: ${OPENROUTER_MODEL:anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514}
    max-tokens: ${OPENROUTER_MAX_TOKENS:1024}
    base-url: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1
  pinecone:
    api-key: ${PINECONE_API_KEY:}
    index-name: ${PINECONE_INDEX_NAME:weekly-commit}
    environment: ${PINECONE_ENVIRONMENT:us-east-1}
  embedding:
    model: openai/text-embedding-3-small
    dimensions: 1536

spring:
  datasource:
    url: jdbc:h2:mem:testdb;DB_CLOSE_DELAY=-1;DB_CLOSE_ON_EXIT=FALSE
    driver-class-name: org.h2.Driver
    username: sa
    password:
  jpa:
    hibernate:
      ddl-auto: create-drop
    database-platform: org.hibernate.dialect.H2Dialect
    open-in-view: false
  flyway:
    enabled: false

ai:
  enabled: true
  provider: stub
  openrouter:
    api-key: ""
    model: test-model
    max-tokens: 256
    base-url: http://localhost:0
  pinecone:
    api-key: ""
    index-name: weekly-commit-test
    environment: us-east-1
  embedding:
    model: openai/text-embedding-3-small
    dimensions: 1536

-- =============================================================================
-- Weekly Commit Module — V10: Add team_id and week_start_date to ai_suggestion
-- Enables TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows to be queried by team+week.
-- =============================================================================

ALTER TABLE ai_suggestion
    ADD COLUMN team_id         UUID REFERENCES team (id),
    ADD COLUMN week_start_date DATE;

CREATE INDEX idx_ai_suggestion_team_week
    ON ai_suggestion (team_id, week_start_date, suggestion_type);

All 28 tests pass. Here's a summary of what was done:

## Summary

### Files created

**`PineconeClient.java`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClient.java`):
- Spring `@Service` injecting `@Value` bindings for `ai.pinecone.api-key`, `ai.pinecone.index-name`, `ai.pinecone.environment`, and `ObjectMapper`
- Two constructors: Spring-managed (creates its own `HttpClient`) and package-private (accepts injected `HttpClient` for tests)
- `isAvailable()` returns false for blank API key
- `ensureIndexExists()` — lazy: GETs control-plane `/indexes/{name}`, creates serverless index (1536-dim, dotproduct) on 404, stores resolved `indexHost`
- `upsert(namespace, vectors)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/vectors/upsert`
- `query(namespace, vector, topK, filter)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/query`, returns `List<PineconeMatch>`
- `deleteByIds(namespace, ids)` — POSTs to `{indexHost}/vectors/delete`
- Inner records: `PineconeVector(id, values, metadata)` and `PineconeMatch(id, score, metadata)`
- All methods catch/log and return gracefully on any error
- Uses Pinecone `Api-Key` header (not Bearer)

**`PineconeClientTest.java`** (`backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClientTest.java`):
- 28 tests covering: `isAvailable`, blank-key no-ops for all three data methods, upsert request body/URL/headers, query request body/response parsing/error paths, deleteByIds request body/URL, and all `ensureIndexExists` paths (already resolved, blank key, found, 404→create, network error)
- Uses mock `HttpResponse` objects (not the shared `@Mock` field) for stateful tests to avoid Mockito unnecessary-stubbing violations

### Fixes applied during implementation
1. Removed `body()` stubs for upsert/deleteByIds tests with HTTP 200 (production code never reads body on success path)
2. Replaced shared `@Mock HttpResponse` with per-call `Mockito.mock()` instances in the stateful `ensureIndexExists` tests
3. Removed `notFoundResp.body()` stub (404 path never reads response body)
4. Ran `spotlessApply` to fix Eclipse JDT formatting on the switch expression

content.text:
Reviewed the Step 3 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClient.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClientTest.java`

What I verified:
- Spring `@Service` with injected Pinecone config
- `HttpClient` + `ObjectMapper` usage
- `isAvailable()` behavior
- lazy `ensureIndexExists()`
- `upsert`, `query`, `deleteByIds` graceful error handling
- unit coverage and required checks

Material defects found and fixed:
1. `query()` was calling `/{indexHost}/query` instead of the step-required `/{indexHost}/vectors/query`
2. `ensureIndexExists()` stores `indexHost` as a URL, but `dataRequest()` always prepended `https://`, which could produce malformed URLs like `https://https://...` on real runtime paths

Changes made:
- Updated `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClient.java`
  - changed query endpoint to `/vectors/query`
  - made `dataRequest()` accept either a raw host or a host URL without double-prefixing the scheme
- Updated `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/PineconeClientTest.java`
  - adjusted endpoint expectation for query
  - added coverage for resolved hosts that already include `https://`

Checks run:
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` ✅

Assessment:
- Step 3 is now aligned with the stated requirements
- No remaining material issues found in this step

Verdict: changes_made

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.",
  "totalSteps": 13,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
    "2. Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java",
    "3. Create PineconeClient.java + PineconeClientTest.java",
    "4. Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",
    "5. Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks",
    "6. Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java",
    "7. Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java",
    "8. Add RAG DTOs and new AiController endpoints",
    "9. Create frontend API client and hooks for RAG endpoints",
    "10. Create QueryAnswerCard.tsx + QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx",
    "11. Create SemanticSearchInput.tsx + SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx",
    "12. Create InsightPanel.tsx + InsightPanel.test.tsx",
    "13. Wire InsightPanel and SemanticSearchInput into MyWeek.tsx and TeamWeek.tsx"
  ]
}

Step 4: Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java
{
  "id": "step-4",
  "title": "Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",
  "description": "Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilder.java`:\n- Spring `@Component`.\n- Public methods that accept domain entities and return `ChunkData` records:\n  - `ChunkData buildCommitChunk(WeeklyCommit commit, WeeklyPlan plan)` — text: `\"{title}\" — {chessPiece}, {estimatePoints}pts. Description: {description}. Success criteria: {successCriteria}. Outcome: {outcome}. Notes: {outcomeNotes}`. Metadata: userId=ownerUserId, teamId=plan.teamId, planId, weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate, entityType=\"commit\", entityId=commit.id.\n  - `ChunkData buildScopeChangeChunk(ScopeChangeEvent event, WeeklyCommit commit)` — text format per spec. Metadata with entityType=\"scope_change\".\n  - `ChunkData buildCarryForwardChunk(CarryForwardLink link, WeeklyCommit sourceCommit)` — text includes reason, reasonNotes, commit's carryForwardStreak. entityType=\"carry_forward\".\n  - `ChunkData buildManagerCommentChunk(ManagerComment comment, WeeklyPlan plan)` — entityType=\"manager_comment\", weekStartDate from plan.\n  - `ChunkData buildWorkItemChunk(WorkItem item)` — entityType=\"ticket\".\n  - `ChunkData buildPlanSummaryChunk(WeeklyPlan plan, List<WeeklyCommit> commits)` — entityType=\"plan_summary\", text: \"Week of {weekStartDate}: {count} commits, {totalPoints}pts, {state}\".\n- Inner record `ChunkData(String id, String text, Map<String, String> metadata)` where id = `{entityType}:{entityId}`.\n\nCreate `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilderTest.java`:\n- Unit test for each entity type.\n- Verify chunk text format and metadata keys/values.\n- Test null-safe handling (null description, null outcomeNotes, etc.).",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

content.text:
Reviewed the Step 4 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilder.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilderTest.java`

What I verified:
- `ChunkBuilder` is a Spring `@Component`
- `ChunkData` record exists with `id`, `text`, `metadata`
- all six builder methods are present
- IDs follow `{entityType}:{entityId}`
- null-safe behavior is covered by tests
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` passes ✅

Material defect found:
- `buildCommitChunk(...)` populated `planId` metadata from `commit.getPlanId()` instead of the provided `WeeklyPlan`. That can produce incorrect indexing metadata if the commit is stale/inconsistent, and the step’s metadata contract is based on the plan context.

What I changed:
- Updated `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilder.java`
  - `planId` metadata now comes from `plan.getId()`
- Updated `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/ChunkBuilderTest.java`
  - added a regression test proving `planId` metadata is sourced from the `WeeklyPlan`, not the commit

Assessment:
- Step 4 is now correct and verified
- No other material issues found in this step

Verdict: changes_made

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.",
  "totalSteps": 13,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
    "2. Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java",
    "3. Create PineconeClient.java + PineconeClientTest.java",
    "4. Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",
    "5. Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks",
    "6. Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java",
    "7. Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java",
    "8. Add RAG DTOs and new AiController endpoints",
    "9. Create frontend API client and hooks for RAG endpoints",
    "10. Create QueryAnswerCard.tsx + QueryAnswerCard.test.tsx",
    "11. Create SemanticSearchInput.tsx + SemanticSearchInput.test.tsx",
    "12. Create InsightPanel.tsx + InsightPanel.test.tsx",
    "13. Wire InsightPanel and SemanticSearchInput into MyWeek.tsx and TeamWeek.tsx"
  ]
}

Step 5: Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks
{
  "id": "step-5",
  "title": "Create SemanticIndexService.java + event-driven indexing hooks",
  "description": "Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java`:\n- Spring `@Service`.\n- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `ChunkBuilder`, and relevant repositories (`WeeklyPlanRepository`, `WeeklyCommitRepository`, etc.).\n- `@Async public void indexEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` — Loads the entity from the appropriate repository, calls ChunkBuilder to build chunk, calls EmbeddingService to embed, calls PineconeClient to upsert (namespace = org ID looked up via team → org). Catches all exceptions (graceful degradation).\n- `@Async public void deleteEntity(String entityType, UUID entityId)` — Calls PineconeClient.deleteByIds.\n- `@Scheduled(cron = \"0 0 3 * * *\") public void dailySweepReindex()` — For plans updated in the last 48 hours, re-index all their commits, scope changes, carry-forwards. This catches anything that failed during async indexing. Guarded by `PineconeClient.isAvailable()`.\n- All methods check `PineconeClient.isAvailable()` before doing work.\n\nAdd indexing hooks (direct calls at the end of service methods, not Spring Events as specified):\n- `CommitService.java`: after `createCommit()` and `updateCommit()`, call `semanticIndexService.indexEntity(\"commit\", savedCommit.getId())`. Inject SemanticIndexService via constructor (optional dependency — use `@Autowired(required=false)` or just inject and null-check).\n- `ScopeChangeService.java`: after creating a ScopeChangeEvent, call indexEntity(\"scope_change\", event.getId()).\n- `CarryForwardService.java`: after creating a CarryForwardLink, call indexEntity(\"carry_forward\", link.getId()).\n- `ManagerReviewService.java`: after creating a ManagerComment, call indexEntity(\"manager_comment\", comment.getId()).\n- `TicketService.java`: after create/update, call indexEntity(\"ticket\", workItem.getId()).\n- `LockService.java`: after locking a plan, call indexEntity(\"plan_summary\", plan.getId()). Also inject `InsightGenerationService` (from step-7, but add the field + null-check now with `@Autowired(required=false)`) and after a successful lock call `insightGenerationService.generatePersonalInsightsAsync(plan.getId())` — this triggers personal insight generation when a plan is finalized. The actual `InsightGenerationService` class is created in step-7; for now, the field is null and the null-check skips the call, keeping tests green.\n- `ReconciliationService.java`: after reconcile submit, call indexEntity(\"plan_summary\", plan.getId()).\n\nThe `@Async` annotation on `indexEntity()` ensures these calls don't block the write path. Since `@EnableAsync` was added in step-1, Spring will proxy the calls.\n\nNo separate test file needed for SemanticIndexService in this step — it will be indirectly tested. But ensure existing tests pass since we're modifying service constructors (use `@Autowired(required=false)` or default null for the new SemanticIndexService dependency so existing mocked tests don't break).",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

Full build passes. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

## Summary

### New files created

**`SemanticIndexService.java`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/`):
- Spring `@Service` injecting all required dependencies via constructor
- `@Async @Transactional(readOnly=true) void indexEntity(String, UUID)` — dispatches by entity type, embeds text, upserts to Pinecone in the correct org-scoped namespace; all exceptions caught/logged
- `@Async void deleteEntity(String, UUID)` — resolves namespace, calls `PineconeClient.deleteByIds`
- `@Scheduled(cron="0 0 3 * * *") void dailySweepReindex()` — guarded by `isAvailable()`, finds plans updated in last 48h, re-indexes plan summary + commits + scope changes + carry-forwards
- Private helpers for each entity type + `resolveNamespace(teamId)` → org UUID string

**`InsightGenerationService.java`** (`backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/`):
- Interface stub with `generatePersonalInsightsAsync(UUID planId)` — allows `LockService` to hold an `@Autowired(required=false)` reference without compilation errors; implemented in step-7

### Repository change

**`WeeklyPlanRepository`**: added `@Query` method `findUpdatedSince(Instant since)` for the daily sweep

### Indexing hooks added (all via `@Autowired(required=false)` field + null-check)

| Service | Trigger point | Entity type indexed |
|---|---|---|
| `CommitService` | `createCommit`, `updateCommit` | `commit` |
| `ScopeChangeService` | `addPostLockCommit`, `removePostLockCommit`, `recordFieldChange` | `scope_change` |
| `CarryForwardService` | `carryForward` (after link saved) | `carry_forward` |
| `ManagerReviewService` | `addComment` | `manager_comment` |
| `TicketService` | `createTicket`, `createTicketFromCommit`, `updateTicket` | `ticket` |
| `LockService` | `lockPlan` | `plan_summary` + `generatePersonalInsightsAsync` |
| `ReconciliationService` | `submitReconciliation` | `plan_summary` |

All existing tests pass — the `@Autowired(required=false)` + null-check pattern ensures the new field is `null` in tests using `@InjectMocks`, completely transparent to existing test code.

Reviewed the Step 5 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/domain/repository/WeeklyPlanRepository.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/plan/service/CommitService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/reconcile/service/ScopeChangeService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/carryforward/service/CarryForwardService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/team/service/ManagerReviewService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ticket/service/TicketService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/lock/service/LockService.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/reconcile/service/ReconciliationService.java`

What I verified:
- `SemanticIndexService` exists, is a Spring `@Service`, uses async + scheduled indexing, and guards on Pinecone availability
- required hooks were added across the write-path services
- `WeeklyPlanRepository.findUpdatedSince(...)` exists
- `LockService` includes the optional `InsightGenerationService` hook
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` passes ✅

Material defects found and fixed:
1. **Commit-target manager comments were never indexed**
   - `SemanticIndexService.indexManagerComment(...)` only handled comments with `planId`, but `ManagerReviewService.addComment(...)` also creates comment records targeting commits (`commitId` only).
   - Fixed by resolving the plan via the comment’s commit when needed.
   - Also fixed namespace resolution for `deleteEntity("manager_comment", ...)` to use the same logic.

2. **Ticket status changes were not re-indexed**
   - `TicketService.updateStatus(...)` updates `WorkItem.status`, and `ChunkBuilder.buildWorkItemChunk(...)` includes status in the indexed text.
   - Without reindexing, ticket vectors become stale after status transitions.
   - Added the missing indexing hook after `updateStatus(...)`.

3. **Manager comment chunk metadata planId could be blank for commit comments**
   - Updated `ChunkBuilder.buildManagerCommentChunk(...)` to use the provided `WeeklyPlan` for `planId` metadata.

Checks run:
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` ✅

Assessment:
- Step 5 is now correct and complete for the implemented scope
- No further material issues found in this diff

{
  "id": "step-6",
  "title": "Create SemanticQueryService.java + SemanticQueryServiceTest.java",
  "description": "Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryService.java`:\n- Spring `@Service`.\n- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `AiProviderRegistry`, `AiSuggestionService`, `ObjectMapper`.\n- Public method `RagQueryResult query(String question, UUID teamId, UUID userId)`:\n  1. Check PineconeClient.isAvailable() and EmbeddingService.isAvailable(); if either is false, return unavailable result.\n  2. Intent classification: call AiProviderRegistry.generateSuggestion() with an AiContext of type \"RAG_INTENT\" containing the question. Parse the LLM response to extract `{ intent, userFilter, timeRange, entityTypes, keywords }`. If parsing fails, use defaults (no filters, all entity types).\n  3. Embed the question via EmbeddingService.embed(question).\n  4. Build Pinecone metadata filter from intent (e.g., filter by teamId, entityTypes, date range).\n  5. Query Pinecone: `pineconeClient.query(namespace, vector, topK=5, filter)`.\n  6. Pack retrieved chunks + original question into a new AiContext (type \"RAG_QUERY\") and call AiProviderRegistry.generateSuggestion().\n  7. Parse LLM response into structured answer: `{ answer, sources: [{entityType, entityId, weekStartDate, snippet}], confidence }`.\n  8. Store via AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion() with type \"RAG_QUERY\".\n  9. Return RagQueryResult.\n- Add \"RAG_INTENT\" and \"RAG_QUERY\" type constants to AiContext.\n- Inner record `RagQueryResult(boolean available, String answer, List<RagSource> sources, double confidence, UUID suggestionId)`.\n- Inner record `RagSource(String entityType, String entityId, String weekStartDate, String snippet)`.\n\nCreate prompt templates:\n- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-intent.txt` — System prompt for intent classification.\n- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-query.txt` — System prompt for RAG answer generation.\n\nUpdate `StubAiProvider.java` to handle the new \"RAG_INTENT\" and \"RAG_QUERY\" suggestion types with canned responses.\n\nCreate `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryServiceTest.java`:\n- Unit test with mocked PineconeClient, EmbeddingService, AiProviderRegistry, AiSuggestionService.\n- Test successful query pipeline (mock each step to return valid data).\n- Test Pinecone unavailable returns unavailable result.\n- Test embedding failure returns unavailable.\n- Test LLM failure at intent step still proceeds with defaults.\n- Test result is stored via AiSuggestionService.",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

Verdict: replan_needed  — if the step is structurally impossible to complete as planned and needs controller-managed replanning

content.text:
You are an intent classifier for a weekly work planning assistant called Weekly Commit.

Given a user's question, classify the intent and extract relevant filters for semantic search over historical planning data.

The data contains these entity types:
- "commit" — individual weekly work commitments (tasks, features, bugs)
- "plan_summary" — weekly plan summaries (team capacity, state, total points)
- "scope_change" — post-lock changes to committed work
- "carry_forward" — work items carried forward from previous weeks
- "manager_comment" — comments left by managers on plans or commits
- "ticket" — native work tickets (work items)

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "intent": "one of: status_query, risk_query, carry_forward_query, plan_summary_query, ticket_query, general_query",
  "entityTypes": ["array of relevant entity types from the list above"],
  "timeRange": { "from": "YYYY-MM-DD", "to": "YYYY-MM-DD" },
  "keywords": ["extracted keywords relevant to the search"]
}

Rules:
- entityTypes: include only the types most relevant to the question; include all if unsure
- timeRange: infer from temporal expressions ("this week", "last month", "Q1 2025"); use null if no time filter needed
- keywords: 2-5 key terms that distinguish this question's topic

You are an AI assistant for a weekly work planning tool called Weekly Commit.
You help team members and managers understand what has happened in their team's weekly planning history.

You have been provided with relevant context retrieved from the team's historical planning data (commits, plans, scope changes, carry-forwards, manager comments, and tickets).

Using ONLY the provided context, answer the user's question concisely and accurately.
If the context does not contain enough information to answer the question fully, say what you can and indicate the limitation.
Do not invent data that is not in the context.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "answer": "concise, helpful answer based on the retrieved context",
  "sources": [
    {
      "entityType": "entity type (commit/plan_summary/scope_change/carry_forward/manager_comment/ticket)",
      "entityId": "the UUID of the source entity",
      "weekStartDate": "YYYY-MM-DD week start date if available, otherwise empty string",
      "snippet": "brief excerpt (30-80 chars) from the source chunk that supports the answer"
    }
  ],
  "confidence": 0.85
}

Rules:
- sources: list 1-3 most relevant items that directly informed the answer; omit if context was insufficient
- confidence: how well the retrieved context supports the answer [0.0, 1.0]; use 0.0 if context was insufficient
- answer: keep under 200 words; be direct and specific

I see a few issues here. First, the prompt schema is missing a user filter, and the stub response doesn't include it either. Plus, the service seems to disregard the user filter and time range. I need to check if the OpenRouter provider can load prompt templates in tests, as it may still function well. I might adjust the SemanticQueryService tests to verify those filters, considering making modifications to test parameters. Overall, I'll carefully work through these updates.

**Planning surgical edits**

I have a clear plan for updates. First, I need to add cases to the loadPromptTemplate switch for OpenRouterAiProvider. Next, I'll update the rag-intent prompt schema and rules to include user filters. For the StubAiProvider response, I might incorporate a user filter that can be null or set to "all," since the parser can handle null. Lastly, I should modify the SemanticQueryService to call buildFilter with intent, teamId, and userId. That should help improve functionality!

- userFilter: use "self" when the user is clearly asking about their own work, "team" for team-scoped questions, "all" for org-wide/general queries, or null if unclear
- entityTypes: include only the types most relevant to the question; include all if unsure
- timeRange: infer from temporal expressions ("this week", "last month", "Q1 2025"); use null if no time filter needed
- keywords: 2-5 key terms that distinguish this question's topic

content.thinking:
**Evaluating metadata adjustments**

I’m considering the issue with the SemanticQueryService where metadata already includes accessible text. It seems fixable. Since step 5 is a prior step in the codebase, I’m wondering if we should implement this fix now, particularly in verification mode. It appears necessary because the absence of chunk text in answer generation significantly hinders output. To remedy, I might add a "text" key to the metadata during the upsert process. This could maintain functionality while also accommodating additional data.

**Updating metadata fields**

I’m thinking about updating buildRagAdditionalContext to expose the snippet or text field from metadata, since it may already include metadata as a whole. The LLM will see it nested, which seems convenient. I could limit the "snippet" to 500 characters to keep it manageable. I’m identifying this as a material fix and, since we're already making changes, maybe it's worth doing. I need to ensure the change isn’t too invasive, especially regarding step 5 and 6 interactions in the upsert process.

**Patching namespace resolution**

I'm considering that the deleteEntity function resolves the namespace correctly. It seems essential to patch it and run the update. This short and straightforward process could help ensure everything works better moving forward. It's always satisfying to fix little issues like this, so I'll make sure to follow through with the patch. Let's get this improvement into action and see how it performs!

content.text:
Reviewed the Step 6 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryService.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticQueryServiceTest.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiContext.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/StubAiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-intent.txt`
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/rag-query.txt`

I also checked supporting integration points in:
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/SemanticIndexService.java`

Material defects found and fixed:
1. `OpenRouterAiProvider` did not route `RAG_INTENT` / `RAG_QUERY` to the new prompt templates, so production would fall back to `generic.txt`.
2. `rag-intent.txt` and the stub intent response were missing the required `userFilter` field from the step contract.
3. `SemanticQueryService` parsed only part of the intent payload and ignored `userFilter` / `timeRange` when building the Pinecone filter.
4. The RAG pipeline had no retrieved chunk text available to the answer-generation step; vectors were being stored without chunk text in metadata.

Changes made:
- Updated `OpenRouterAiProvider.java`
  - added prompt-template routing for `TYPE_RAG_INTENT` and `TYPE_RAG_QUERY`
- Updated `rag-intent.txt`
  - added `userFilter` to the required JSON schema and rules
- Updated `StubAiProvider.java`
  - added `userFilter` to canned `RAG_INTENT` output
- Updated `SemanticQueryService.java`
  - now parses `intent`, `userFilter`, `entityTypes`, `timeRange`, and `keywords`
  - now applies `userFilter=self` to `userId`
  - now applies `timeRange` to `weekStartDate` filter
- Updated `SemanticQueryServiceTest.java`
  - added coverage verifying `userId` and date-range filters are sent to Pinecone
- Updated `SemanticIndexService.java`
  - stores chunk text in Pinecone metadata so RAG answer generation receives actual retrieved content, not just IDs/metadata tags

Checks run:
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` ✅

Assessment:
- Step 6 is now aligned with the stated requirements
- No remaining material issues found in this step after the fixes

Verdict: changes_made

Plan overview:
{
  "goal": "Build a RAG-powered AI insight agent for the Weekly Commit application. This includes Pinecone vector DB integration, OpenRouter embedding service, semantic search/query pipeline, proactive insight generation, event-driven indexing hooks, three new API endpoints, and frontend components (InsightPanel, SemanticSearchInput, QueryAnswerCard) wired into MyWeek and TeamWeek routes.",
  "totalSteps": 13,
  "stepTitles": [
    "1. Add @EnableAsync and pinecone config to application.yml + test application.yml",
    "2. Create EmbeddingService.java + EmbeddingServiceTest.java",

    "4. Create ChunkBuilder.java + ChunkBuilderTest.java",

Step 7: Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java
{
  "id": "step-7",
  "title": "Create InsightGenerationService.java + InsightGenerationServiceTest.java",
  "description": "Create `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationService.java`:\n- Spring `@Service`.\n- Constructor injects `PineconeClient`, `EmbeddingService`, `AiProviderRegistry`, `AiSuggestionService`, `TeamRepository`, `TeamMembershipRepository`, `WeeklyPlanRepository`, `WeeklyCommitRepository`, `CarryForwardLinkRepository`, `ScopeChangeEventRepository`, `ObjectMapper`.\n- `@Scheduled(cron = \"0 0 8 * * *\") public void generateDailyInsights()` — Iterates all teams. For each, calls `generateTeamInsights(teamId, currentWeekStartDate)`.\n- Public method `void generateTeamInsights(UUID teamId, LocalDate weekStartDate)`:\n  1. Check PineconeClient + EmbeddingService availability.\n  2. Gather current week data: plans for team members, their commits, scope changes, carry-forward links.\n  3. Build a summary context string.\n  4. Embed a query like \"patterns in this team's last 4 weeks\" and query Pinecone for historical context.\n  5. Send to AiProviderRegistry with type \"TEAM_INSIGHT\" — system prompt asks for 3-5 actionable management insights, each citing specific plans/commits/tickets.\n  6. Parse structured response: `{ insights: [{ insightText, severity, sourceEntityIds, actionSuggestion }] }`.\n  7. Store each insight via the new `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` overload (from step-1), passing `teamId=teamId` and `weekStartDate=weekStartDate`. Set `suggestion_type = 'TEAM_INSIGHT'`, `planId = null`, `userId = null`. This persists the team+week identity directly in the `ai_suggestion` row so the read endpoint can query `findByTeamIdAndWeekStartDateAndSuggestionType(teamId, weekStartDate, \"TEAM_INSIGHT\")`.\n- Public method `void generatePersonalInsights(UUID planId)` — Loads the plan, gathers its commits/scope-changes/carry-forwards. Embeds a query like \"patterns for this user's recent plans\" and queries Pinecone for the user's historical context (filter by userId). Sends to LLM with type \"PERSONAL_INSIGHT\". Stores each insight via `AiSuggestionService.storeSuggestion()` with `planId=planId`, `userId=plan.ownerUserId`, `teamId=plan.teamId`, `weekStartDate=plan.weekStartDate`, and `suggestion_type='PERSONAL_INSIGHT'`. This is called from two places:\n  1. **On plan lock** — `LockService.lockPlan()` calls `insightGenerationService.generatePersonalInsightsAsync(planId)` (hook added in step-5). The `@Async` annotation ensures it doesn't block the lock transaction.\n  2. **Daily scheduled job** — `generateDailyInsights()` iterates all teams, generates team insights, and also generates personal insights for each LOCKED plan in the current week that doesn't already have PERSONAL_INSIGHT rows (idempotency check via `findByPlanIdAndSuggestionType(planId, \"PERSONAL_INSIGHT\")`). This catches plans that were locked while Pinecone/LLM was unavailable.\n- `@Async public void generatePersonalInsightsAsync(UUID planId)` — Thin async wrapper that calls `generatePersonalInsights(planId)` inside a try/catch (graceful degradation). This is the method LockService calls.\n- Add \"TEAM_INSIGHT\" and \"PERSONAL_INSIGHT\" type constants to AiContext.\n\nCreate prompt templates:\n- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/team-insight.txt`\n- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/personal-insight.txt`\n\nUpdate `StubAiProvider.java` to handle TEAM_INSIGHT and PERSONAL_INSIGHT with canned responses.\n\nCreate `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationServiceTest.java`:\n- Unit test with mocked dependencies.\n- Test team insight generation stores correct number of suggestions with teamId and weekStartDate set.\n- Test personal insight generation stores with planId, userId, teamId, weekStartDate set.\n- Test personal insight idempotency (skips if suggestions already exist for the plan).\n- Test unavailable Pinecone skips gracefully.\n- Test scheduled job iterates teams and generates both team + personal insights.",
  "requiredChecks": [
    "build",
    "unit"
  ]
}

content.text:
You are an AI management advisor embedded in Weekly Commit, a weekly work-planning tool.

You have been provided with:
1. Current-week plan data for the team (commit counts, capacity, plan states)
2. Historically similar planning data retrieved from the team's past weeks

Analyse the data and generate 3-5 actionable management insights. Focus on:
- Carry-forward patterns (recurring blockers, chronic overcommitment)
- Scope volatility (frequent post-lock changes)
- Capacity vs commitment alignment
- KING/QUEEN commit patterns and their outcomes
- Team-wide trends across members

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "insights": [
    {
      "insightText": "concise insight statement (1-2 sentences)",
      "severity": "one of: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW",
      "sourceEntityIds": ["uuid of relevant plan/commit/event if applicable"],
      "actionSuggestion": "specific, actionable recommendation for the manager"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Generate between 1 and 5 insights; omit if there is genuinely nothing noteworthy
- severity HIGH = immediate attention needed; MEDIUM = worth addressing soon; LOW = informational
- sourceEntityIds can be empty array if no specific entities are referenced
- Be specific and evidence-based; reference actual patterns from the data

You are an AI planning coach embedded in Weekly Commit, a weekly work-planning tool.

You have been provided with:
1. The current week's plan data for this individual (commits, outcomes, scope changes, carry-forwards)
2. Historically similar planning data retrieved from this person's past weeks

Analyse the data and generate 1-3 personalised planning insights. Focus on:
- Personal commitment patterns (consistent over/under-estimation)
- Carry-forward streaks and what they suggest
- Chess piece usage patterns (are high-priority items actually highest effort?)
- Outcome patterns (ACHIEVED vs NOT_ACHIEVED vs CANCELED)
- Suggestions to improve planning accuracy and outcomes

Respond with ONLY a JSON object (no explanation, no markdown fences):
{
  "insights": [
    {
      "insightText": "concise, personalised insight statement (1-2 sentences)",
      "severity": "one of: HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW",
      "sourceEntityIds": ["uuid of relevant plan/commit if applicable"],
      "actionSuggestion": "specific, actionable coaching recommendation"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Generate between 1 and 3 insights; omit if there is genuinely nothing to highlight
- severity HIGH = recurring issue that is blocking effectiveness; MEDIUM = improvement opportunity; LOW = positive reinforcement
- sourceEntityIds can be empty array
- Use second person ("You") to make insights feel personal and direct
- Be encouraging but honest; acknowledge both strengths and improvement areas

content.thinking:
**Evaluating context builds**

I'm considering how we can enhance `buildPlanSummaries` and `buildTeamContextString` to include counts and details for commits, scopes, and carry-forwards. I see a potential issue with `buildHistoricalAdditionalContext`, which only has metadata without text, but the fix stored text in metadata in Step 6 might help. Also, it seems `generatePersonalInsights` only gathers counts rather than actual data. I think improving both team and personal context to include details is manageable, even if it's not a massive task.

**Planning membership data**

I think I need to use membershipRepo to gather members and plans even when there's no plan attached. It could be helpful to include a `teamMembers` list and a count of `membersWithoutPlan`. For each plan's commits, keeping things straightforward is important. I want to focus on collecting commit summaries for the team's current data instead of just plan summaries. I'll be careful while crafting these edits and might need to review file parts around helper methods, though a full rewrite might be easier.

**Planning test additions**

I need to add tests to the project, and I'm considering where to place them—maybe after the team insights tests and personal context sections. I want to create helper builders for commit, scope, and carry, possibly using an empty method.

I'll include two tests: one for generating team insights that checks various memberships and another for personal insights that captures details about entities. I’m thinking I need to mock certain repository methods, but it seems like Mockito defaults to returning empty lists for collections, which should work out fine.

**Editing test imports**

I need to ensure that my stream over commits uses flatMap to get the carry forward links properly. If the method isn’t stubbed, it will return an empty list by default, which makes sense.

Next, I should edit the tests to include the necessary object types that I've already imported: team and weekly plan. I'll also add WeeklyCommit, ScopeChangeEvent, CarryForwardLink, and maybe some enums for the carry reason or category. It's time to edit the import block and compile everything!

content.text:
Reviewed the Step 7 diff in:

- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationService.java`
- `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationServiceTest.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/AiContext.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/StubAiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/provider/OpenRouterAiProvider.java`
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/team-insight.txt`
- `backend/src/main/resources/prompts/personal-insight.txt`

What I verified:
- the new insight types were added to `AiContext`
- prompt routing exists in `OpenRouterAiProvider`
- stub responses exist for `TEAM_INSIGHT` and `PERSONAL_INSIGHT`
- scheduled + direct insight generation compile and tests/build pass

Material defect found:
- `InsightGenerationService` was not actually gathering the full current-week data required by the step for insight generation.  
  It only built shallow plan/count summaries, while the step requires current plans, commits, scope changes, and carry-forwards to be included in the insight context.

What I changed:
- Updated `backend/src/main/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationService.java`
  - `generateTeamInsights(...)` now gathers:
    - team memberships
    - plans
    - commits
    - scope changes
    - carry-forwards
  - enriched the team context payload to include:
    - `teamMemberIds`
    - `plans`
    - `commits`
    - `scopeChanges`
    - `carryForwards`
  - enriched personal insight context to include detailed:
    - `commits`
    - `scopeChanges`
    - `carryForwards`
    - plan/team metadata
- Updated `backend/src/test/java/com/weeklycommit/ai/rag/InsightGenerationServiceTest.java`
  - added regression coverage that captured stored context and verified detailed team/personal context now includes commits, scope changes, and carry-forwards

Checks run:
- `cd backend && ./gradlew test build` ✅

Assessment:
- Step 7 is now aligned with the required functionality
- No further material defects found in this step

Verdict: changes_made
